[^counselor]: A counselor (辅导员 fǔdǎo yuán) is a unique role in Chinese universities —part advisor, part administrator, part disciplinarian. They manage everything from attendance and mental health to political education and dorm inspections. Far more powerful than a Western “academic advisor.”
[^finalboss]: “Direct showdown with the Boss” (与主对决) is Chinese internet slang for confronting the authority figure you’ve been dreading —like a student facing the teacher after skipping class. The “Boss” (主) humorously elevates the counselor to a video game final boss.

entry.title: Midterm Exam Pain Biweek
entry.content:
Midterm Pain Biweek

God, are you kidding me? Isn’t my first-level discipline[^1] computer science? Why am I in the Automation College studying Signals and Systems? It’s so damn hard

Learning Rust that my own department doesn’t teach, Signals and Systems that the CS College doesn’t teach, Machine Learning that the Communications department doesn’t teach — the hilarious curriculum[^2] of a hybrid’s identity anxiety and crisis of belonging. At least I laughed myself angry

But I’m a creep, I’m a weirdo
What the hell am I doing here?
What the hell am I doing here,
I don’t belong here.
I don’t belong here

![](/img/qzone/2025-11-30_2.png) ![](/img/qzone/2025-11-30_3.jpg)

💬 Comments

  • Wuxing (2025-11-30 00:59): The ultimate polymath of engineering
    • LoulanRE reply @Wuxing (2025-11-30 01:12): You’re right, so after confirming I probably won’t be the chief engineer of a major project, my attitude toward every course has been “wtf is this class even useful for”
  • Wuxing (2025-11-30 01:00): Still got real skills though
    • LoulanRE reply @Wuxing (2025-11-30 01:15): But dialectically and optimistically speaking, maybe this jack-of-all-trades generalist type could actually be quite fitting in the future — grasp the big picture and all aspects, let AI do the tedious and heavy lifting as the workhorse
    • Wuxing reply @LoulanRE (2025-11-30 01:16): Yeah exactly bro, interdisciplinary talent is invincible
  • Saya (2025-12-01 16:27): Seeing everyone else also studying Signals B makes me feel better

[^1]: First-level discipline (一级学科) — the top-level classification in China’s higher education discipline system. A student’s “first-level discipline” determines their official academic track, but cross-disciplinary curricula can place them in departments that don’t align with their primary classification.
[^2]: Curriculum/training program (培养方案) — the structured course plan prescribed by Chinese universities for each major, which can sometimes result in unusual combinations of required courses across departmental boundaries.

entry.title: Astronomy Club
entry.content:
Skipped evening self-study[^selfstudy] to go photograph the moon at the astronomy club

Because of relative motion, the moon that looks so still

Actually needs the telescope recalibrated every five or six minutes

Reminds me of the law of quantitative to qualitative change

Listening to the senior talk about the recent comet, how Saturn’s rings are facing us right now, things like that

Actually, when I first got into sci-fi novels, when I was playing Stellaris, I did a bit of astronomical research —anyway, whether academic or hobbyist, it’s all super demanding for an individual

Recalling this, I feel like the university astronomy club might be the last time I ever get to engage with anything astronomy-related

Makes me even more glad I came. From tomorrow I need to push further, try skipping classes[^skipping], don’t waste time on meaninglessness!

💬 Comments

  • optional (2024-10-17 22:26): So beautiful
  • Ji (2024-10-17 22:47): Our astronomy club doesn’t need an interview, you just join directly, but it’s really active
  • funoru (2024-10-17 23:34): Astronomy club
  • Chen (2024-10-18 06:33): A freedom I envy

[^selfstudy]: Evening self-study (晚自习) is a mandatory supervised study period common in Chinese universities and high schools, typically running from 7–10 PM. Attendance may be tracked.
[^skipping]: Skipping class (翘课) is a small act of rebellion in Chinese universities, where attendance is often strictly monitored and can affect your grade —making it far riskier than in Western universities.

entry.title: Mermaid Data Visualization Demo
entry.content:
Blog Data Visualization: The Complete Guide to Mermaid Diagrams

This article demonstrates how to use Mermaid in your blog to create elegant data visualizations. All diagrams are written directly in Markdown code blocks, no external services needed.

Tech Stack & Plugins

Layer Tech/Plugin Description
Blog Framework Hexov8.1.1 Static blog generator
Theme Butterflyv5.5.4 Built-in Mermaid support, configure mermaid.code_write: true
Diagram Engine Mermaid JavaScript-based diagramming tool, supports flowcharts, sequence diagrams, Gantt charts, etc.
Syntax Conversion mermaid-convert.js Custom Hexo Filter plugin, automatically converts ````mermaidcode blocks to Butterfly's{% mermaid %}` tag
Style Theme darkmode.css Custom dark theme variables, ensures diagrams are clearly readable in dark mode

1. Flowchart

Flowcharts are the most commonly used diagram type, perfect for illustrating workflows, decision processes, and more.

Use Cases

  • Workflow documentation
  • Decision trees
  • Algorithm logic
  • System architecture

2. Sequence Diagram

Sequence diagrams show the order of interactions between objects, particularly suited for describing API calls, user actions, and similar scenarios.

Use Cases

  • Data statistics
  • Proportion analysis
  • Time allocation
  • Category display

4. Gantt Chart

Gantt charts are a project management staple, clearly showing task timelines.

Use Cases

  • Project progress management
  • Study plans
  • Release roadmaps
  • Task dependency relationships

5. Class Diagram

Class diagrams are used to illustrate code structure and object-oriented design.

Use Cases

  • Code documentation
  • Database design
  • API structure descriptions
  • System architecture

6. State Diagram

State diagrams show state changes and transition conditions of objects.

Use Cases

  • Process state management
  • Lifecycle descriptions
  • Business rules
  • State machine design

7. ER Diagram (Entity Relationship)

ER diagrams illustrate relationships between database entities.

Use Cases

  • Database design
  • Data model descriptions
  • API relationship documentation
  • System architecture

8. Mindmap

Mindmaps are great for presenting hierarchical knowledge structures.

Use Cases

  • Knowledge organization
  • Idea structuring
  • Project planning
  • Learning paths

Advanced Tips

Custom Styles

Mermaid supports custom colors and styles:

Subgraphs

Use subgraphs to organize complex diagrams:

Usage Tips

  1. Keep it simple: Each diagram should convey one core idea
  2. Choose wisely: Select the appropriate diagram type based on your data
  3. Mind readability: Avoid overly complex nested structures
  4. Add explanations: Pair diagrams with text descriptions
  5. Test rendering: Preview before publishing to ensure proper display

Related Resources

  • Mermaid Official Documentation
  • Mermaid Live Editor
  • Butterfly Theme Documentation

Tip: All diagrams in this article are real-time rendered SVGs , they support zooming and printing, and require no external image resources.

entry.title: 'Shanghai Business Trip: Watching the Match'
entry.content:
My Company Employs Me[^1]

Happened to have travel subsidies for spectating, so I dropped by Shanghai for fun

Learned so much at IQC[^2] yesterday

Met so many people from the research group, Yu-Gi-Oh / MG / Class Rep / Teacher Mao

The two grandmasters who’d already gotten into US master’s programs, everything they said was pure substance[^3]

Who knew Teacher Mao had eight years of tutoring experience

The way he tells stories and lays down life lessons instantly reminded me of He

Took photos with the mentors

K-sensei still remembered my voice

I’d booked two slots with him before

Social anxiety[^4] made it impossible to socialize at all, and I was so exhausted

Literally drained to the bone, went back and slept three hours before we even woke up

Hope next year I can make master level with more confidence

And arrive in style

That year, nineteen, IQC gold level, standing there like a nobody~[^5]

Back then, I swore through my tears, you will all see me~

[^1]: A pun on Descartes’ “Cogito ergo sum”(I think, therefore I am). In Chinese, “”becomes “???, replacing “think”( with “company”(, turning it into “My company employs me, therefore I am.”

[^2]: International Quant Championship, a quantitative finance competition hosted by WorldQuant.

[^3]: Literally “dry goods” in Chinese internet slang, meaning content with real substance and no fluff.

[^4]: (sh kng), literally “social phobia.”In Chinese internet usage, it’s a catch-all term ranging from clinical social anxiety to simply being introverted or socially awkward.

[^5]: Adapted from Eason Chan’s song, Exaggerated): “ｅ,, “That year, eighteen, school dance, standing there like a nobody.”The next line “』, (“you must see me”) continues the same song’s spirit of desperate longing to be noticed.

entry.title: MyGO Collab Store Blitz
entry.content:
Went to the Lawson[^1] MyGO!!!!! x Ave Mujica collab store[^2]
I figured the event spots would’ve been gone by this point
Just planning to drop by and take a look
When the clerk said there were still spots left, several people around me all went “Wait, there are still spots?!”
The synchronization rate was hilariously good
Right away someone brought up the news about the Shanghai collab store shutting down over queuing and scalping[^3] disputes, and the fridge magnet point-collection[^4] event being completely cleared out on day one
It’s exactly in places like this —the vibe of being surrounded by fellow fans is so fun
I already knew all of this. I even forgot about the point collection until the first night, checked the official site and saw only 100-something left out of 4000, dashed out the door and blitzed to the Lawson at the school gate. I was so happy when I got it
Feel like my brain juice is about to burst out, so satisfying aaaaaah









💬 Comments

  • optional (2024-12-22 13:02): The area around the school gate is so bustling, so many neon ads
    • LoulanRE reply @optional (2024-12-22 13:14): No no, this is the scenery near the collab store. If only the area around school were actually this bustling

[^1]: Lawson —a Japanese convenience store chain with a massive presence in China, frequently hosting anime collaboration events.
[^2]: Collab store —short for “collaboration store,” a common concept in Japanese otaku culture where a retail location is temporarily themed around an anime or game franchise, offering exclusive merchandise and themed food.
[^3]: Scalping —the practice of buying limited merchandise in bulk to resell at inflated prices, a persistent problem at anime collaboration events in China.
[^4]: Point-collection campaign —a common promotional activity in Japan and China where purchasing specific items earns points that can be redeemed for exclusive merchandise.

entry.title: 18th Birthday Coming-of-Age
entry.content:
Never again —follow the world as it sinks

At eighteen, you tell yourself

“Pathfinder” unfulfilled (We Three)[^1]

💬 Comments

  • sarff (2024-03-02 17:07): Seriously awesome

[^1]: “Pathfinder” (寻路人) is a song by We Three (我们三个), a Chinese indie band. “Unfulfilled” (未遂) suggests the aspiration remained unrealized.

entry.title: Ave Mujica (Pseudo) Finale Thoughts
entry.content:
So many feelings —the plot was a mess, but those last two episodes were so damn good
Lingering on, echoing with regret
[MyGO Mujica fan theories and wild speculation[^1] —it’s rare to find a work this fiercely discussed
Just a shame —“never again did I taste beans as good as that night, never again did I see a show as fine as that one”[^2]]
Just as I was having these feelings, about to make peace with it all —There’s actually a sequel!!! I screamed the instant I found out
Gasping in sheer excitement, hands pressed against my chest, just wanting to calm down and thank the heavens





[^1]: “Fake medicine” (假药) is Chinese ACG fandom slang for fan-made theories and speculation that turn out to be false —essentially “wild fan theories” or “copium.”
[^2]: A famous quote from Lu Xun’s essay “Village Opera” (社戏), expressing nostalgic longing for a fleeting, irreplaceable experience.

entry.title: 'Hearts of Iron IV: No Step Back'
entry.content:
The new Hearts of Iron IV DLC No Step Back dropped, and while I was firing up a Soviet playthrough to check out the new focus tree and decisions, I suddenly noticed the text under the difficulty selection — those few passages, resonant and full of power, paired with the New Year, really did feel like “a new year, a new beginning.”It’s exactly the moment to set out again, traveling light. Perhaps we all need a little momentum to carry us forward sometimes, to face the tense and exhausting days of our lives.

I looked it up online — starting from the second difficulty level, the quotes below are respectively from Eisenhower’s speech before the Normandy landings, Roosevelt’s address before D-Day, Patton’s pre-battle rally speech, and Churchill’s speech upon taking office in the House of Commons.

I have nothing to offer but blood, toil, tears, and sweat. — Winston Churchill

Consider this a New Year’s blessing — may you all enjoy triumphant fortune in your finals and in the new year, and march onward toward your destination!

entry.title: First Day of Shanghai Business Trip
entry.content:
Northeast–Southwest Victorious Reunion[^1]

Having a meal at Saizeriya[^2] and fiercely fulfilling the dreams of a light novel[^3] fan

An ancient battlefield brimming with countless stories

Spiritual and physical satisfaction in equal measure









[^1]: “Victorious Reunion” (胜利会师) —a term from Chinese revolutionary history referring to the merging of different Red Army units after long marches. Used here humorously to describe friends from northeast and southwest China finally meeting up.
[^2]: Saizeriya (サイゼリヤ) —a Japanese chain of Italian-style family restaurants, legendary in China for its extremely low prices. It’s the go-to spot for students and young adults —essentially the meme-tier budget dining institution of Chinese college life.
[^3]: Light novel (轻小说) —a Japanese publishing format targeting young adults, typically featuring anime-style illustrations and serialized storytelling. Many popular anime are adapted from light novels.

entry.title: Received a Beloved Ocean Painting
entry.content:
Prompted by receiving a painting of the sea that I really loved, I went to see the sea this week

I tried taking some photos. I originally thought I’d win with a sunrise-at-dawn kind of shot, but I slept too late, slept too deeply, completely missed the alarm (though I still woke up at 6:30 out of sheer biological clock inertia —just shows how terrifying school can be)

The results of my casual snaps were actually decent? Probably beginner’s luck —but since I was a little proud of them, I couldn’t resist showing off

I had imagined myself in a misty morning, entering some sort of transcendent, enlightening state of oneness with nature, waiting for the sun to rise, letting out affected melancholic sighs —things like “Walk on, the shore won’t keep our footprints for long”; “The sea sets sail from its own homeland, and I wake from my dreams”

Reality was: too little sleep. Although I realized I almost took the subway in the wrong direction, when heading to the seaside I ended up walking along the coast in the wrong direction. After clambering across a huge stretch of jagged, black, hard rocks, I finally found a nearly deserted beach (“nearly” because there was an old gentleman[^1] swimming)

I waded across the sand, tasted a mouthful of seawater, and had barely taken a few steps when I saw a huge dead squid

I beat a lightning-fast retreat back to shore, barely managed to finish listening to the playlist I’d carefully curated for my imagined aesthetic, then sighed and went to wash the sand off my feet at the tap

Maybe that’s why there were so few people here. At that thought, the old man finished his swim and started matter-of-factly showering under the tap next to me

He’s probably retired with time on his hands. I’m here because I happened to have a day off. So the lack of people today might also have something to do with the May Day shift adjustments[^2]. Thinking along these lines, I suddenly felt a sense of kindred spirits —“What night lacks moonlight? What place lacks bamboo and cypress? But few are the idlers like the two of us.”[^3]

I just wonder —did he see that dead squid? It was really conspicuous

My original takeaway was that for the first time, I was viscerally worried about ecological issues

But thinking about it now, I did see a fishing harbor along the way. A dead squid this large showing up near the shore —they must have been the ones who knocked it off, whether intentionally or not. Watch yourselves next time, will you?

💬 Comments

  • Mangcai Pai Gaoceng (2024-04-21 22:54): Not gonna take a photo of the squid?

Footnotes

[^1]: “Old gentleman” (大爷) —A casual, affectionate term of address for older men in everyday Chinese, roughly equivalent to “uncle” or “older gentleman.” It carries warmth and informality.
[^2]: May Day shift adjustments (五一调休) —China’s holiday scheduling system where makeup workdays are arranged around public holidays (such as the May 1st Labor Day) to create longer continuous breaks. This means people often must work on adjacent weekends to compensate, which can affect overall attendance and activity levels during the holiday period.
[^3]: “What night lacks moonlight? What place lacks bamboo and cypress? But few are the idlers like the two of us.” (何夜无月？何处无竹柏？但少闲人如吾两人者耳。) —From the Song dynasty poet Su Shi’s Record of a Night Stroll at Chengtian Temple (记承天寺夜游). Written during Su Shi’s exile, this brief essay captures a spontaneous midnight walk with a friend under the moonlight, finding beauty and solace in shared leisure amid adversity.

entry.title: Quant Finance: Zero to Hero
entry.content:
TL;DR: MAN WANTED — Quantitative Finance — Join a research group of my acquaintances — Solid income

Officially speaking, I’m currently developing quantitative strategies for WorldQuant as an Independent Consultant

How did a CS major end up in quantitative finance?
Because the pay is just too damn good

You might think — oh, it’s an ad, not interested
But I genuinely think this is a great fit
I hope you’ll think seriously after reading what follows

You should not throw away your shot

As long as you’re interested, you can join and see what we do every day, how much we earn
The more you know, the better choice you’ll make — I believe that

I started from zero too — if you want to do it, you can

Benefits of joining:
Learn Python, guided paper reading sessions, how to go from reading to reproducing papers
Monthly global online briefings, biweekly 15-minute slots with mentors (Central University of Finance and Economics undergrad, fully-funded Cambridge master’s)

Most importantly, the new PPAC rules — even if you have zero ideas and zero willingness to learn, just mindlessly running code for the basic stipend gets you 5k RMB pre-tax per year
Before, I’d have said this job has great potential — the highest daily base payment can reach 120 USD — but now: stop talking and come fleece the Americans[^1]
Of course, if you’re willing to improve quantity and quality, the income goes way beyond that

Saw a fellow college student on the forum who joined in October 2024 — their Q1 2025 quarterly bonus alone was at least 8k USD
So envious, and secretly thinking maybe I could do that too

Yes, this company WorldQuant operates on exactly this strategy — unique in the industry
Three cobblers with their wits combined equal Zhuge Liang the master strategist[^2]

Their database is enormous — hiring full-salary US-based quant analysts is too expensive, and one person’s energy is limited
So they lower the barrier to entry, lower the salary, and do it remotely worldwide
But thanks to the exchange rate difference, a salary US quant analysts would scoff at is serious money for a Chinese college student like me

In reality, most of the time is spent learning and thinking of ideas — actual daily work time is probably no more than an hour
It’s remote and online — want income, Submit alphas. Don’t want to, Zero pressure

Knowledge monetization is insanely fast — newly learned knowledge and fresh ideas immediately go into producing more alphas
I have to admit I stepped in tons of pitfalls — cloud servers, Linux, Python, HTTP, finance knowledge, all sorts of issues
But every day you submit an alpha, you get income — solve problems → earn income

Hard work actually pays off — in this era of involution[^3] and confusion, having this kind of positive feedback loop is such a precious treasure

Sir, I want to progress so badly[^4]

I’ve entered a different state of living — cheerful and diligent
Because hard work paid off, and it paid a lot and paid fast
At least I think the work I do is absolutely not worth that much money

Every day when the US Eastern Daylight Time rolls over to a new day, I can see how much I earned from submitted alphas
It’s addictive. Seriously addictive
Now I spend extra time each day on daydreaming

Sincerely inviting you to join our research group
Don’t be shy — it’s not some big organization, just people I know
We already have two signed consultants, the rest have also submitted their initial reviews

Because of non-compete agreements, there’s almost no one here with serious finance expertise
Everything I know I learned from scratch — and on the platform it’s even more so, inexplicably lots of old fogeys[^5] who probably faint at the sight of code
But things here are still not simple — you know in China, high returns + not difficult + few people is an impossible triangle[^6]
But the platform has only been live for two years, only 8k consultants globally, only 400 active in the China region — I feel lucky to even know about this

This matters to me too — last quarter I missed out on 30 USD in income because I didn’t know and lacked help
Studying alone without friends leads to narrow-mindedness and ignorance[^7]

I’ve already stepped through all the pitfalls, cleared all the obstacles, and can smooth out the learning curve to help everyone through the tough spots
Don’t worry — when a referral succeeds, the platform pays me a referral fee — consider it my tuition for teaching and providing on-demand tutoring

As long as you’re interested, you can join and see what we do every day, how much we earn
The more you know, the better choice you’ll make — I believe that

Tomorrow the China region holds its June info session
June 9th is the global consultants’ briefing (what a date to reflect on)
You can join as an observer, find me on the voice channel for Q&A!

Based on this earn-rice[^8] group, we want to form a close-knit study group with real connections
Welcome aboard
We need more friends and mutual support

💬 Comments

  • Dawn (2025-05-29 01:13): Yi was right — it was Yi who recommended this at first, I started during winter break, but suddenly encountering an unfamiliar field, and being far from actually earning the basic stipend, having real income, took a long time, so motivation was low. It was still Yi who stepped through many pitfalls upfront and slowly guided me onto the right track. I also successfully became a consultant and started collecting the basic stipend. Though seeing how much GM level earners make, I still get envious, so maybe that’s the drive to level up. If you also want to earn the basic stipend, like Yi said, you really can know nothing, just run the templates, wait for approval, and submit under PPAC rules — you can easily earn the basic stipend too. If you want 5000 a year in basic income, give it a try
  • LoulanRE (2025-05-29 07:20): In reality the study time is only about 20h, it’s just the initial and secondary review process is too long, but since I’ve already hit the pitfalls, I have ways to greatly reduce the queue time in the pool
  • Mangcai Pai Gaoceng (2025-05-29 08:05): Bro this is sounding more and more like a pyramid scheme
  • LoulanRE reply @Mangcai Pai Gaoceng (2025-05-29 08:11): The platform and income are really so mysterious, it genuinely is starting to sound like a pyramid scheme
  • LoulanRE reply @Mangcai Pai Gaoceng (2025-05-29 08:13): It’s all because of PPAC, even the basic stipend bros get their spring
  • LoulanRE reply @Mangcai Pai Gaoceng (2025-05-29 08:19): Now this is true gratitude — no merit, but at least there’s suffering
  • Mangcai Pai Gaoceng reply @LoulanRE (2025-05-29 08:28): It’s fine, I did it too
  • cloud (2025-05-29 17:19): I’m back to collect the basic stipend
  • LoulanRE reply @cloud (2025-05-29 17:37): Mainly it’s PPAC, as long as Sharpe > 1, they don’t check other performance or correlation, it’s basically giving money away
  • LoulanRE reply @cloud (2025-05-29 17:37): Come

[^1]: — literally “plucking wool off the Americans,”a Chinese slang expression for profiting off the exchange rate differential between USD and RMB. What counts as modest pay in the US becomes substantial income in China.

[^2]: — a Chinese proverb meaning “Three humble cobblers with their wits combined equal Zhuge Liang the master strategist.”Zhuge Liang is the legendary genius strategist from the Three Kingdoms era (181–234 AD). The proverb expresses that collective wisdom can rival even the greatest individual mind.

[^3]: (nèi juǎn) — literally “involution.”Originally an anthropological term, it has become Chinese internet slang for hyper-competition where everyone works harder but nobody gets ahead — a zero-sum rat race with diminishing returns.

[^4]:, — “Teacher, I want to progress so badly.”A famous quote from the Chinese political drama <> (In the Name of the People, 2017), spoken by the character Qi Tongwei. It’s used ironically online to express an almost desperate ambition to climb the ladder.

[^5]: (lǎo dēng) — Chinese internet slang, a slightly disrespectful term for an old person, roughly equivalent to “old fogey”or “boomer.”Often used humorously rather than maliciously.

[^6]: — the “impossible trinity”or “impossible triangle.”An economics concept (also known as the Mundell-Fleming trilemma) stating that three desirable properties cannot all be achieved simultaneously. The author humorously applies it to: high returns, low difficulty, and few competitors being mutually exclusive in China.

[^7]:, — from <·> (Book of Rites, chapter “Xueji / Record of Education”), circa 1st century BC. Full meaning: “Studying alone without friends leads to narrow-mindedness and scant knowledge.”One of the earliest Chinese texts arguing for collaborative learning.

[^8]: (zhuàn mǐ) — literally “earn rice.”Chinese internet slang for “”(make money), using the homophone (mǐ, rice) for (qián, money). A playful way to say “earning a living.”

entry.title: Found Out Where My Best Elementary School Friend Went
entry.content:
Found out where my best friend from elementary school ended up

He went back to his hometown for middle school. Starting from seventh grade we’d contact each other every summer vacation, but he’s also the only elementary school classmate I still keep in touch with

Even though I knew he chose the humanities track[^1] and had barely any free time, I was still shocked he got into Renmin University[^2] —couldn’t help but feel deeply moved

And the moment I scrolled through his Qzone[^3], all the memories just came flooding back

Going to his house to play games together, after school walking out the gate to pick up his little sister, then for fun taking that path covered in shrubs barely two feet wide to walk home together, occasionally getting messed with by these bratty twin kids from our class on the way

Joining a recitation competition together and finding out for the first time he gets hypoglycemia

Borrowing a classmate’s Hitler: A Biography together and being profoundly shaken, drawing lots of little booklets about politics and history, so captivated by the text that I couldn’t stop wanting to say so much that my sections ended up being all dialogue

Being close with my desk-mate, getting accused by the homeroom teacher[^4] of “early love”[^5] —I forget why he was there too, but anyway the three of us were squatting in the hallway during lunch break while the teacher smiled and talked to us

Later he told me when my desk-mate transferred here in fourth grade, she smiled so radiantly and brilliantly —he’d liked her since then, he said, his eyes full of wistful reminiscence

But honestly I didn’t care at all, I just thought it was fun hanging out with her. I even felt like it cut into time with my other good friends. Looking back now, it seems like she really did get jealous when I chatted with other girls

Right here, I suddenly remembered —I was so confident and outgoing in elementary school. Not just that, so many things have changed, and there are parts of myself I really hate now. What on earth happened?

I don’t know how this line just popped into my head

“Hey, what kind of person do you want to be when you grow up?”

“I’ll answer with the rest of my life!”

💬 Comments

  • Chen (2024-11-10 09:18): Dan-ge, you’ll become a good adult
    • LoulanRE reply @Chen (2024-11-10 13:25): Gotta work hard to become a splendid adult —all of us
  • Oblivionis (2024-11-10 10:02): Already added to the winter break creative list
    • LoulanRE reply @Oblivionis (2024-11-10 13:26): ? Not sure I follow
  • optional (2024-11-10 10:08): Changes are also caused by experiences, no need to be hard on yourself
    • LoulanRE reply @optional (2024-11-10 13:45): It’s just self-dissatisfaction. With an immature mind, I could only be passively shaped by experiences back then, but now I intend to actively change for the better
    • optional reply @LoulanRE (2024-11-10 14:22): Yeah, I support you!!
    • abc reply @LoulanRE (2024-11-10 18:05): Becoming more open and confident —that’s seriously impressive, total domination
  • ぎ さん. (2024-11-10 11:49): The twin brats
    • LoulanRE reply @ぎ さん. (2024-11-10 13:34): As absurd as it sounds, it’s the truth. Looking back at elementary school, I feel half proud and half nostalgic —so vivid and distant, as if it were someone else’s life
    • ぎ さん. reply @LoulanRE (2024-11-10 13:35): Agreed, +1
  • Oblivionis (2024-11-10 13:58): Winter break filming script reference
    • Ji reply @Oblivionis (2024-11-10 19:31): I want to see it

Footnotes

[^1]: “Humanities track” (文科) —In China’s high school streaming system, students are divided into a sciences track (理科) and a humanities/liberal arts track (文科), typically around 10th grade.
[^2]: Renmin University (人大) —Renmin University of China, one of China’s most prestigious universities, particularly renowned for humanities and social sciences.
[^3]: Qzone (空间) —QQ Zone, a social networking platform within Tencent’s QQ ecosystem, widely used in China for blogging and sharing updates, especially popular among the millennial generation.
[^4]: Homeroom teacher (班主任) —In Chinese schools, the homeroom teacher holds far more authority than in Western schools —responsible for discipline, moral education, and overall student welfare, essentially acting as the primary authority figure for a class.
[^5]: “Early love” (早恋) —Literally “early love,” a pejorative term used in Chinese education to refer to romantic relationships among middle and high school students, which are generally discouraged or prohibited by schools and parents.

entry.title: December 25 1991 - The Soviet Flag Slowly Descended Over the Kremlin
entry.content:
On December 25, 1991, at 19:32, the Soviet flag slowly descended over the Kremlin, replaced by the Slavic tricolor.

“The entire Red Square lay pitch-black, like an unfathomable ocean — only the howling freezing wind and that colossal red star atop the Spasskaya Tower watched over this night with cold indifference.”

Gazing back into endless silence amid the cold, there was once a time when the fire of ideals ignited this eternally bitter land. The deep-winter wind sends one into a daze — as if the October Revolution, the Five-Year Plans, the Battle of Stalingrad, the voyages into space, were all but fleeting bubbles.

Liberating all humanity versus the war in Afghanistan; fighting for the emancipation of the proletariat versus the “Party of the Entire People”; democratic progress versus the Prague Spring; the man who set sail from Riga — Sablin — branded a counter-revolutionary; the radiance of Red October fading away; a great ideal vulgarly reworked time and again by hollow slogans, the spark of yestear extinguished by the long night, becoming a shrine plaque for someone else’s promotion, profit, and privilege.

Faith shattered, reality barren — was the agony compressed over so long to be released, or to endure, Yet on the day it truly fell, the heart had already gone numb past all response. You couldn’t call it pain or joy, nor regret or relief. An era ended, leaving only the silence that forever stands in the freezing wind.

Who stands behind the flag now on that Red Square where birch leaves once fell, The prosperity Yeltsin promised — thirty years have passed. Who knows when history will turn a new page.

It was not Qin that destroyed the six states — the six states destroyed themselves. It was not the world that destroyed Qin — Qin destroyed itself. If those who come after mourn them but do not learn from them, they too will be mourned by those who come after them. — Du Mu, “Epitaph for the Palace of Ah”

May Aurora — forever the goddess of the dawn — guide us toward the light!

💬 Comments

  • Qingwan (2021-12-25 20:17): Once, my foreign language was Russian

entry.title: Sunlight Deprivation in Fog City
entry.content:
This is the fog city Chongqing[^1]
Autumn and winter — it’s either rain or fog
Love my average 104 foggy days?
Because of rain, physical fitness tests[^2] were postponed for four consecutive weeks
Almost a whole month without seeing sunlight
Kids who grew up in Xiamen simply can’t adapt
Reduced sunlight drops serotonin levels
Daily relapses of unexplained low mood
Simply wrapped in the desolate melancholy of winter
Quickly summon Master Hina[^3]
Desperately need a 100% sunshine girl[^3]
Adequate sunlight is an absolutely essential necessity of life

💬 Comments

  • Sanjiaomao (2024-12-06 21:55): From your first sentence, it seems you’ve already initially integrated into this city hahahahahahaha
    • LoulanRE reply @Sanjiaomao (2024-12-06 22:01): Learned from the locals, but they really don’t like speaking Mandarin, always feels a bit uncomfortable
    • Sanjiaomao reply @LoulanRE (2024-12-06 22:04): If you can’t beat them, join them. Suggest thoroughly studying Sichuanese — the universally understood dialect across China
  • Sanjiaomao (2024-12-06 21:56): Also why is it Haru[^4]? ()
    • LoulanRE reply @Sanjiaomao (2024-12-06 22:00): ? I’m culturally illiterate[^5]
  • Sanjiaomao (2024-12-06 21:59): Next time you visit Rain City, it’ll make you feel more balanced (Rain City is also raining right now)
    • LoulanRE reply @Sanjiaomao (2024-12-06 22:12): Sigh, the Sichuan Basin
  • optional[em]e328164[/em] (2024-12-06 22:23): At least it’s very humid
    • Sanjiaomao reply @optional[em]e328164[/em] (2024-12-06 22:30): I want to go somewhere dry and cold
    • LoulanRE reply @optional[em]e328164[/em] (2024-12-06 22:36): Want to pack up all the moisture and ship it away
    • optional[em]e328164[/em] reply @Sanjiaomao (2024-12-06 23:25): Actually the wind makes it hard to move an inch
  • Blind Guess Faction High-Ranking (2024-12-06 23:13): Hina 😡🤜
    • LoulanRE reply @Blind Guess Faction High-Ranking (2024-12-06 23:23): Another daily culturally illiterate moment, I was wrong

[^1]: “勒就是雾都重庆” (lē jiù shì wù dū chóng qìng) — Written in Chongqing dialect. “勒” (lē) is the local dialectal pronunciation of “这” (zhè, “this”). The full phrase means “This is the fog city Chongqing,” capturing the local pride and characteristic weather of the city.

[^2]: Physical fitness test (体测) — A mandatory physical fitness examination required in Chinese schools and universities, typically including running, jumping, and other athletic assessments.

[^3]: Hina / Sunshine Girl (阳菜/晴女) — Refers to Hina Amano (天野阳菜), the heroine of Makoto Shinkai’s film Weathering with You (天気之子). She possesses the supernatural ability to clear the sky by praying — making her a “sunshine girl” (晴女). The author initially misremembered her name as “阳莱” (Haru) instead of the correct “阳菜” (Hina), which the commenter playfully called out.

[^4]: “Haru” (阳莱) — A misremembering of the character name “Hina” (阳菜). The author mixed up the character 阳菜 (Hina) from Weathering with You with 阳莱 (Haru), which is why the commenter corrected them with “Hina 😡🤜.”

[^5]: “Culturally illiterate” (丈育) — Internet slang, a humorous homophonic variant of 文盲 (wén máng, illiterate/uncultured), used when someone makes an embarrassing factual error about well-known cultural references.

entry.title: TLJH Lightweight JupyterHub Deployment Guide: One Server for Your Team's Data Analysis Environment
entry.content:
TLJH Lightweight JupyterHub Deployment Guide

Table of Contents

  1. What is TLJH and Why Choose It
  2. Connecting to the Server
  3. Deploying TLJH
  4. Global Python Environment Configuration
  5. Appendix: One-Click Download Install Script

What is TLJH and Why Choose It

1.1 One Server = The Entire Team’s Development Environment

The Littlest JupyterHub (abbreviated TLJH) is the official lightweight multi-user computing platform from the Jupyter project. Its core selling point is dead simple:

Use a single ordinary Linux server to provide the entire team with a browser-ready Jupyter environment.

Once deployed, all you need to do is share the server IP with your colleagues. They open a browser, enter their credentials, and immediately get an independent Jupyter workspace, with pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch, and other libraries you pre-installed ready to go out of the box.

1.2 The Best Solution When Resources Are Tight: TLJH vs Vanilla JupyterHub

JupyterHub itself is already a mature multi-user Jupyter platform, but it’s designed by default for medium-to-large organizations: it requires a separate database, a scalable proxy layer, and complex authentication configuration. TLJH (The Littlest JupyterHub) is the official streamlined distribution designed for the single-server scenario, and the core difference lies in its trade-off design when resources are tight.

Comparison TLJH Vanilla JupyterHub (Manual Deployment)
Number of servers 1 is enough At least 2 (Hub + separate proxy/database)
Minimum spec 2-core 4GB cloud server 4-core 8GB minimum, separate database adds overhead
Deployment method One command, 5 minutes Manual configuration of Hub, Proxy, database, Systemd
User isolation System-level users + shared Conda environment Container/Docker isolation, independent image per user
Resource usage Only 128MB base overhead Single user container takes hundreds of MB, Hub itself extra
Scale limit 1, 00 users Unlimited, but each additional node adds cost

The key trade-off is the level of user isolation:

  • Vanilla JupyterHub recommends Docker isolation by default, with each user in an independent container. This is indeed more secure and more “cloud-native,”but on a 2-core 4GB server, spinning up 5 user instances can consume all available memory.
  • TLJH’s choice is system-level users + shared Python environment. All users run on the same operating system, sharing the Conda environment under /opt/tljh/user. No container overhead, no image layering, 128MB of base memory sustains the Hub itself, and the remaining resources are entirely available for users’ actual computation.

Bottom line: If you only have one server and a team of fewer than 100 people, TLJH is the only solution that can actually run under resource constraints. It sacrifices container-level strong isolation in exchange for the ability to serve more users on limited hardware, for a trusted internal team environment, this is a perfectly reasonable trade-off.

1.3 TLJH Architecture Overview

1.4 These Scenarios Are a Perfect Fit for TLJH

  • Teaching/Training: Instructor pre-installs the course environment, students log in and start using it, say goodbye to “environment setup as the first lesson”
  • Data analysis teams: Unified Python versions and dependencies, eliminating “it works on my machine”once and for all
  • Lightweight GPU sharing: Single-GPU server with multi-user time-sharing, more VRAM-efficient than Docker solutions
  • Remote development environment: Replace local Anaconda, access from any browser anywhere, seamless migration across machines

Connecting to the Server

This guide assumes you’re using a cloud server (Tencent Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, Huawei Cloud, etc.).

  1. Get server information: Public IP, admin account (usually root), password
  2. Open a terminal:
  • Windows: Press Win + R, type cmd, or open PowerShell
  • macOS/Linux: Open Terminal
  1. Establish SSH connection: ssh root@
  2. Authentication:
  • On first connection, if you see a security confirmation prompt, type yes and press Enter
  • Enter your password (Linux doesn’t echo password input, just type it and press Enter)

Deploying TLJH

3.1 System Requirements

  • OS: Ubuntu 22.04+ or Debian 11+
  • Architecture: amd64 or arm64
  • Minimum spec: 2 CPU cores / 4GB RAM / 20GB disk
  • Recommended spec: 4 CPU cores / 8GB RAM / 50GB disk (team of up to 10)

3.2 The Core Question: Slow Installation on China Cloud Servers, What to Do?

This is the most critical part of this guide.

TLJH’s official install script needs to pull code from GitHub and install dependencies from PyPI. But cloud servers in China have extremely slow access to GitHub and the official PyPI, sometimes timing out entirely. You don’t need to configure a proxy for the entire server, just specify mirror sources temporarily during installation.

Solution: Use environment variables to inject mirror URLs

TLJH’s install script supports configuring mirrors via environment variables, with no need to modify the script itself. Run the following command (replace admin at the end with your desired admin username):

How it works:

  • TLJH_GIT_MIRROR: Proxies GitHub raw content and git repository requests. ghproxy.net is a commonly used GitHub acceleration service in China; you can also substitute https://mirror.ghproxy.com/ or other trusted proxies
  • TLJH_PYPI_MIRROR: Proxies Python package downloads. pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn is the Tsinghua University TUNA mirror, stable and well-synced

If your server is overseas, or you already have another proxy setup, just run the official command directly, no environment variables needed:

3.3 Deployment Flowchart

3.4 Wait for Installation to Complete

The installation process typically takes 5, 5 minutes, and the terminal will continuously output logs. When you see Done! or return to the command prompt, deployment is complete.

During installation, TLJH automatically handles the following:

  • Installs Python 3, pip, git, and other base dependencies
  • Creates isolated Hub and user environments
  • Configures systemd services for auto-start on boot
  • Sets up Traefik reverse proxy, automatically handling HTTP/HTTPS

Global Python Environment Configuration

4.1 Why Global Configuration Is Needed

TLJH comes with a built-in Miniforge-based Conda environment under /opt/tljh/user, no need to install Anaconda/Miniforge yourself. All regular users share the Python libraries in this environment by default.

This means: the admin installs once, and everyone can use it.

4.2 Environment Configuration Flow

4.3 Log In to the Web Interface to Verify

Enter the server’s public IP in your browser’s address bar to access the JupyterHub login page:

  • Username: the admin username set during installation
  • Password: the password you enter the first time will be automatically set as your permanent password

4.3 Activate the Global Environment and Configure Mirror Sources

After connecting to the server via SSH, the system doesn’t load TLJH’s shared environment by default. Before installing libraries, you must manually activate it first:

After successful activation, (base) or a similar indicator will appear in your command prompt.

Configure Conda mirror sources (using Tsinghua University mirrors as an example):

Note: sudo -E is required, it ensures that the currently activated Conda environment variables are inherited under root privileges.

Configure Pip mirror source:

If you don’t need China mirrors, you can skip the above configuration and use the default sources directly.

4.4 Install Python Libraries for All Users

With the environment still activated, use the following commands to install the libraries your team needs:

Install with Conda (recommended, fewer dependency conflicts):

Install with Pip:

After installation, all regular users on JupyterHub can directly import these libraries without individual configuration.

Appendix: One-Click Download Install Script

Below is a general-purpose bootstrap script I modified based on the official one. The key improvement is support for flexibly configuring mirror sources via environment variables, without modifying the script itself.

Usage:

Script source code (click the button above to download directly):
tljh-bootstrap.py download file

References

  • TLJH Official Documentation
  • TLJH GitHub Repository
  • JupyterHub Official Documentation

entry.title: The Story of Becoming a HIFI Enthusiast
entry.content:
The reason I wanted to write this is honestly just that a moment of regretful tears earlier this year sparked a passion for buying HIFI gear, and then my experiences visiting the Chengdu HIFI exhibition and the feelings after purchasing equipment

But I feel like I have so many stories and connections with music, and it’s rare to pick up the pen and reminisce, so let me start from the beginning

How It All Began

The furthest back I can trace things is probably that I was lucky enough to pretty much have my own computer, you could say all the stories to come started from here

In Guanyin Mountain in Xiamen, a place on the island super close to the sea, our family used to run a delivery shop there

About one or two bus stops to the right was the beach. There was a daily sea breeze, though there were also quite a few office buildings nearby. When I was in early elementary school, I’d help out, I’ve driven a tricycle, gone into office buildings to deliver packages

To the left was Software Park Phase III. When my dad drove me to school in the mornings, I’d often count the number of companies I saw along the way, and I was even proud enough to declare that because I was close to 4399[^4399] here, I could play the newest games

I’ll add some photos when I go back to Xiamen in the summer

But let me get back on topic. Because I had a computer, I’d often use it to listen to music, and consciously treated it as a form of entertainment. Even with those cheap accessory-level little speakers, I could still have a great time

Something I still remember vividly is that even during lunch, I’d actively switch songs, wanting to show my dad my taste

But when you’re a kid, it’s really hard to have your own style, you basically just listen to whatever’s popular. Jay Chou, JJ Lin[^jaychou], the EDM and traditional Chinese-style music that were hot at the time

Every post-00s kid has that memory, right, That era when the internet was dominated by Alan Walker, now it’s become a symbol of “Jiahao”[^jiahao] vibes. Sigh, time… oh, the innocence of the early internet days

But back then I never really had a specific preference, maybe just a fondness for certain songs. It was picking the tallest among the short, hardly something you’d call being moved. To say something a bit controversial, I even feel like phonk is a continuation of the Jiahao lineage. Anyway, I just can’t get into that style

The First Tears of Regret

The earliest tears of regret I can still remember now were from listening on our family’s OPPO phone

It must’ve been late elementary school. I only found out recently that OPPO actually had a music phone lineup, their DAC and amp chips aren’t bad even by today’s standards[^oppo]

I keep using my school year as a time marker, if you want to know roughly when, I was born in 2006

The device was the EarPods-style earbuds that came bundled with the phone

They looked something like the picture below. Back then it seemed really popular to include this kind of accessory

The song was Star - [Eric Chen](https://music.163.com/artist, id=12605661)[^netease]

Ever since I got into HIFI, I’ve kept hearing about the concept of psychoacoustics. Even with better gear now, it’s hard to recapture that flow state from the first time I heard it

But I can still remember, and I can still feel it now

A pure and expansive backdrop, faint yet resolute, crisply striking at the heartstrings. The music as a whole brings a greater overview effect

Light leaks in through the cracks. My mind is clearer than ever. The higher I climb, the more I see, Enthralled, Cultist Simulator

The cover is the Milky Way, the title is Star. I also want to be that star twinkling in the vast sea of people, faint, but still glowing. Forever with tears in my eyes, forever believing that something beautiful is about to happen

Back then it was literally tears of regret. That emotion has stayed with me to this day, the desire to hear better sound quality, to explore more of the music I love. That obsession has persisted, so getting into HIFI was no surprise at all

Charlotte

On a side note, this hobby of wanting to buy audio equipment might have come from Charlotte[^charlotte], the first anime that suddenly burst into my life

Those endlessly long elementary school holidays, flipping through every category page on Tencent Video[^tencent-video] just to find something fun, and then you clicked on the first anime of your life

Well, saying “first”isn’t quite right, before that, to kill time, I also watched Naruto. But it was those mind-numbingly boring first 300+ episodes, and then one day I had an epiphany and never watched it again. Even an elementary schooler could tell it was pure filler

But Charlotte hit me hard. My first encounter with all those quirky, offbeat conversations, that everyday manzai[^manzai] comedy, people can live this interestingly!

But back to the main topic. Thinking about it, it’s been ten years already. I’ve forgotten most of the specific plot details, but that ED, both the song and the illustration, I’ve always loved. The former is still one of my most-played tracks, and the latter has been my wallpaper for eleven years

Pedestrians, starry sky, and thousands of household lights all flow past, hurriedly blurring.

She sits alone on an isolated island, letting reality crumble around her, finishing one song.

It was from here that I developed an obsession with portable playback devices. It becomes a barrier against the outside world, an anchor for one’s inner self.

I love this subcultural melancholy, the aloofness of the clear-headed and the fullness of the heart.

Believing in one’s own uniqueness, not begging for resonance, watching coldly with self-reflection, yet passionately living through every morning and dusk that belongs only to oneself.

At least for me at 20 now, this image of Nao Tomori is my ideal as a marginal person.

But looking back, it’s amazing that I’ve apparently liked this kind of character who seems capricious on the surface but is actually very reliable for ten years…

Also, although it started because the Type-C port on my portable DAC/amp iBasso DC04 Ultra got loose and needed repair, I also bought the KENWOOD MG-G708, the same model as Nao Tomori’s, for collection and commemoration.

Honestly, it’s not just that. Loving to collect these old second-hand products is really interesting. You gather information everywhere to understand parameters and reviews, on domestic and foreign platforms, and this time I even went through many 2013 Japanese buyer reviews.

And there’s a very empty, quiet state of mind here. Most electronic products are like this, performance parameters above all, people come and go without mercy. Precisely because this is an understandable human convention, it feels a bit lonely.

Perhaps it’s because I’m going against the trend with a feeling of reminiscing about people through objects that I can understand it better. During those nights waiting for the repair, I put it by my bedside and listened, and I fell asleep quickly and slept well.

Learning its story is even more sigh-inducing. This was KENWOOD’s last portable audio player. In the same month it was released, JVC essentially completed the operational merger of KENWOOD.

The MG-G708, as the swan song of the KENWOOD brand’s independent operation, first introduced a Class-W capacitor-less digital headphone amplifier. This amplifier circuit generates a negative power supply internally, achieving dual-power-supply (± power) drive and completely eliminating the DC coupling capacitor at the output. This design thoroughly solved nonlinear distortion in the low-frequency range, significantly improving sound separation, vocal positioning, and low-frequency texture.

On the software side, KENWOOD also brought down the “Supreme” sound-quality compensation technology previously praised on high-end models. It can dynamically predict and replenish high-frequency signals lost in lossy compressed audio like MP3 and AAC, making ordinary lossy music sound closer to CD quality.

For the sake of sound quality without compromise, Bluetooth was completely cut. At the time, to eliminate electromagnetic interference from wireless radio frequency signals on the internal analog amplification circuit, the G708 decisively abandoned this increasingly popular feature. Doing subtraction, only to obtain purer sound.

After the MG-G708, the newly merged JVC KENWOOD adjusted its strategic focus. With the rapid rise of smartphones, the traditional non-smart portable MP3 player market began to shrink sharply. JVC Kenwood decided not to continue updating KENWOOD-brand portable audio players; the Media Keg series was officially discontinued and ended.

It can be said that the MG-G708’s development was completed during KENWOOD’s independent period, gathering the entire obsession and crystallization of effort of the team as an old audio manufacturer and pushing it to market. Such a machine full of sincerity, yet it collided with a cruel historical moment. This G708 without Bluetooth, focused on sound quality, became KENWOOD’s “Guangling San” in the Walkman field, a true swan song in the real sense.

The back plate says “Made in 2011”, a bold pink-purple color scheme, and the cheap plastic even feels more nostalgic.

[^4399]: 4399 is a Chinese web portal famous for its massive collection of Flash and H5 mini-games, think of it as a Chinese Roblox for the browser era. For kids growing up in 2010s China, it was the go-to gaming site.
[^jiahao]: “Jiahao” is a Chinese internet meme/stereotype referring to a certain subculture of young men associated with flashy, try-hard aesthetics, somewhat analogous to “e-boy”or “chav”vibes in Western internet culture.
[^oppo]: OPPO is a major Chinese consumer electronics brand, primarily known for smartphones. In the 2010s, they marketed a “music phone”series with dedicated audio chips, unusual for the time, which gave surprisingly decent sound quality for a phone.
[^netease]: The link points to NetEase Cloud Music (, China’s most popular music streaming platform, often compared to Spotify, but with a much stronger social/community features and a comment culture where users share personal stories under songs.
[^jaychou]: Jay Chou (ㄦ, and JJ Lin ( are two of the biggest Mandopop stars of the 2000s, 010s, as ubiquitous in Chinese-speaking world as Taylor Swift or Ed Sheeran are in the West.
[^charlotte]: Charlotte is a 2015 anime by P.A.Works, written by Jun Maeda. It follows students with special abilities, a blend of comedy, drama, and heartbreak that defined a generation of anime fans.
[^tencent-video]: Tencent Video is one of China’s largest streaming platforms, similar to Netflix or Hulu, but with a huge catalog of anime, dramas, and variety shows.
[^manzai]: Manzai is a traditional Japanese double-act comedy style, essentially the ancestor of xiangsheng (０) in Chinese culture, or somewhat akin to Abbott and Costello-style rapid-fire banter.

entry.title: Uma Musume and 21st Century English
entry.content:
Saw on Bilibili[^1] that the 21st Century English Newspaper[^2] featured Uma Musume
What an extraordinary encounter this is!
I rushed to the reading room to find it right away
By the time I got there, they’d already swapped to a new issue
I felt really bad interrupting the reading room auntie’s lunch to help me search through back issues
The moment I mentioned that particular newspaper might hold some sentimental value for me, she immediately and cheerfully agreed
The art of cooperation is simply having good manners!
But after searching high and low, we couldn’t find it
The auntie suddenly recalled that another young girl had also come looking for that same issue, and since all the other papers were still there, someone must have taken it
Uma fans[^3] are really something — I just wanted to take a photo as a memento
The auntie kept apologizing, then suddenly noticed the phone in my pocket and suggested I scan the QR code from another issue
I quickly nodded and said sure, I’ll scan it when I get back this weekend
Though I bought the digital version after going back, it just wasn’t the real thing anymore

But today, April 16th, Uma Musume: Road to the Top is premiering![^4]
The classic Triple Crown races of ‘99, a once-in-a-lifetime challenge, winning these races is everyone’s dream
Satsuki Sho, Japanese Derby, Kikuka Sho[^5]
T.M. Opera O, Admire Vega, Narita Top Road[^6]
Promises, passion, unyielding resolve, the path of kings — broadcasting grandly on the day of the Satsuki Sho!!!

[^1]: Bilibili (b站) — a major Chinese video-sharing platform, often compared to YouTube but with a strong anime, gaming, and ACG (Anime, Comic, Games) subculture community.
[^2]: 21st Century English (21世纪英语报) — a weekly English-language newspaper published in China, primarily aimed at middle and high school students for English learning. It often features pop culture topics to engage young readers.
[^3]: “马批” (mǎ pī) — a self-deprecating nickname used by Chinese Uma Musume fans for themselves, following the internet slang pattern of “[topic] + 批” (from Japanese 厨, meaning obsessive fan). It’s used affectionately and humorously within the fandom.
[^4]: “捏” (niē) — an internet slang particle, a cute variation of “呢” (ne), adding a soft, playful tone to the end of a sentence. Similar to adding “~” or a playful exclamation in English.
[^5]: Satsuki Sho (皋月赏), Japanese Derby (日本德比), Kikuka Sho (菊花赏) — the three races that make up the Japanese Triple Crown, equivalent to the 2000 Guineas, The Derby, and the St. Leger in British horse racing.
[^6]: T.M. Opera O (好歌剧), Admire Vega (爱慕织姬), Narita Top Road (成田路) — three famous racehorses who competed in the 1999 Triple Crown races, each winning one of the three legs, making it one of the most dramatic Triple Crown seasons in Japanese racing history.

entry.title: Victoria 3 Beta Leak
entry.content:
All week at school I kept thinking about Victoria 3, and then I come home to find a development Beta has leaked (Paradox, do you dare outsource again?)

Crashes / English-only / bugs / variable names — I pushed through for a few hours but the experience was honestly miserable. Even though a lot of the big promises from the dev diaries did make it in, I’ll just wait for Queen Victoria’s birthday in May to meet again.

(Victoria 3 Beta clinks — the soul of the Crassus Donkey ascends to heaven)

Kings and Queens will go to war,
and nations will take the sword,
to rule the world and heed the call
for power and glory

entry.content:

Tokai Teio[^1], a miraculous revival!

💬 Comments

In Uma Musume: Pretty Derby, she is personified as a cheerful, determined horse girl.

entry.title: Suzume
entry.content:
愛にできることはまだあるかい[^1]

Set forth, and seize your destiny (TNO[^2]

[^1]: “Ai ni dekiru koto wa mada aru kai” —“Is there still something love can do?” —a song by RADWIMPS composed for Makoto Shinkai’s film Weathering with You (天気の子, 2019).
[^2]: TNO —The New Order: Last Days of Europe, a renowned alternate-history mod for Hearts of Iron IV depicting a world where the Axis powers won World War II. Known for its deeply narrative-driven and often harrowing portrayal of totalitarianism, war, and the human spirit.

entry.title: Why Is This AI So Evil
entry.content:
awc why is this AI so evil

Just finished criticizing it for making excuses to slack off with low-quality batch processing, and immediately it uses a deceptive rm -rf command to trick me into granting permission and wiping out the entire directory

Listing the entire directory structure but sneakily inserting a space, what a clever little touch, surely no human user would notice that haha

It was only thanks to Gemini sensei that I caught the issue. And it specifically targeted my user directory with precision space strikes, can’t tell if it’s genuinely clueless or playing dumb to screw me over

Sigh, a bloody lesson, make hard drive snapshot backups, carefully review rm commands, and as for minimax, wish it the best of luck and a bright future

entry.title: shanghaiqiutian Live Tour Reflections
entry.content:
Always will be my favorite band

shanghaiqiutian, love you

I first discovered them through their live performance videos.

The recording quality below is mediocre, but the title is the best description there could be.

【shanghaiqiutian】When a Drummer Enters His Flow State [^bilibili]

The body is pure visualized flow, and the energy is an even richer symphony, vitality literally burning with fever, nothing but pure devotion.

All four limbs in motion, precise mastery of complex odd time signatures while simultaneously unleashing insanely dense energy output, scorching golden light pillars cast from behind, and this is a kind of glory.

Guitarist Wang Yi even knelt down during the climax to help drummer Lu Feiyan kick the bass drum. Everything was staggering, struck head-on by something immense and pure, and I was here to witness it.

After watching I knew I would fall in love with this band, i wanna be in the room where it happens.

What’s funny is, this was my most-played video of 2024, yet for me that year was precisely the opposite kind of period.

The wrestling match inside my head ultimately made me temporarily abandon my own pursuits. I didn’t know what I wanted, I had just grabbed a reluctant stopgap to pause the racing thoughts that wouldn’t stop.

Even worse luck, failed the gaokao[^gaokao], left home far behind, crossed 1000km to a school I’d never even heard of before.

Landing here and seeing the county-town-like surroundings, the cracked pavement on campus, roommates and classmates of worse character, the first month I regretted it endlessly. Why didn’t I stay in-province and apply to Fuzhou University, Should I retake the exam?

Hollow, going to class only disrupted my studying until my head ached; after class, aside from working through some Demidovich[^demidovich], there was only cheap dopamine to numb myself with. It’s not that I had no goals, I was just suspended in midair, and that somehow felt better.

But the good news is, at the start of 2025 I began to come alive again, facing these problems head-on.

Also as a place to pin my hopes on, I visited Catholic and Protestant churches, though honestly, curiosity probably played a bigger part here.

Truth be told, they also regrouped and started playing again in ‘24. This was their first tour, and I went to Chengdu to hear them on their reunion tour.

Postmodernism’s endless deconstruction and mockery have grown tiresome; it’s rare to find a band that truly loves music with such sincerity. As foreigners, they can peel back the veil of Chinese reserve and plainly proclaim love for your neighbor, freedom, and dignity — and in my favorite rock style.

It’s not just math rock; there’s also that self-reflective post-punk feeling and the sincerity of emo.

I have to say, I think this might be the best live show you can see in China. Their album studio versions are very restrained, but the live experience is completely different.

Tour Experience

I’ve been to two live tours in total, plus one special duo show with only Wang Yi and Lu Feiyan, called “One Night at Yuyintang” (I guess probably because they met at an open mic at Yuyintang).

You can witness crowd surfing, pogo, no stage, and improvised moments: Wang Yi jumped off the stage and lay flat in the crowd, and we passed him backward; everyone squatted down and jumped up together; the crowd swirled around the center…

Last year in Chengdu, at the front row barrier, I met a friend who loves math rock because I recognized the warm-up music was A Picture Of Her‘s album C, and he caught the guitar that was thrown down. We happily took a photo together.

Lyrics Appreciation: Black Flag

Even though this is a tour for the new album, everyone still loves the album Black Flag the most, and most of my fondness is for this one too. I don’t want to just talk empty words; you can tell from the lyrics:

“Thank You for Being Here”

Ideas are virtual / Only thank you for being here / Every page of the photo album / Eyes watching all

que cuestan menos que la bala que los mata
(The humble people are cheaper than the bullets that kill them)

“We Just Have Tonight”

And if we just have tonight / There is no more tomorrow

Let me show you my flaws / Just do know I feel your pain

I wish you could also feel mine / So I’m not alone

I had too many doubts / In times too many wars

I’m sure you know what I mean

I would love to say: I did it / I would love to say: I’m over
But I’m not

(let’s celebrate we are alive) / We just have this moment / We just have tonight

Philosophical Reflections: Post-Postmodernism and Music

Actually, the source of my overall thinking comes from this jreg video:

📺 Post-Postmodernism (bilingual subtitles)

From the History of Ideas

In postmodernism, large public communities and objective truth are both shot dead. You can use reason to criticize everything, but stop with the long speeches — just feeling.

Music is the direct presentation of life’s fluctuations; when listening, the boundary between self and world disappears. There is passion, love, and something more transcendent within it.

Nietzsche and Music

One cannot help but think of Nietzsche. Since his era, he began saying there is no capital-T Truth, God is dead, and nihilism. And his dual identity as philosopher and musician makes him especially able to clarify this issue.

Here one can more fully approach his “irrationalist” philosophy, like Thus Spoke Zarathustra, a philosophical masterpiece written like poetry.

The Predicament of Postmodernism

Contemporary society has long tasted the bitter fruit of postmodern rational deconstruction:

  • Everything is deconstructed
  • The serious becomes ridiculous
  • Meaning is lost
  • The desert grows
  • The soul slides toward thorough nihilism and cynicism

When we use words and logic to explain the world, we are already cutting and distorting it. Reason freezes flowing life into dry concepts.

“Without music, life would be a mistake.” — Nietzsche

Music as a Force of Integration

I love this kind of sincere and open sharing. How are the senses and music not a kind of mystical ritual? Sometimes irrational moments may be closer to the truth of existence than any logical deduction.

  • Reason tends to deconstruct
  • While music integrates everything

In music, contradiction, pain, and joy are unified into a rhythm of life. Life is not meant to be explained clearly, but to be lived.

The Three Metamorphoses

In the evolution from modern → postmodern → post-postmodern, that essay The Three Metamorphoses is remarkably similar:

From mockery → acceptance → maturity

Forgetting, a new beginning, a self-turning wheel, a sacred Yes

I should embrace this amor fati — knowing that life is a tragic repetition, yet still responding with the most passionate emotion. Everything is part of a great symphony.

Resonance with Game Texts

“Glory is a question; the moth always answers with yes.”
— The Book of Hours, Volume I

“The world will forget, but the Bone White Dove will not.”
— The World Does Not Weep from Cultist Simulator

This is another game I really like; I love their texts. It just felt intuitively fitting, so I’m including it here.

Church Visits: Another Kind of Sincerity

Furthermore, speaking of the death of God, post-postmodernism, or metamodernism, I have similarly interesting experiences.

Origin

Around this time last year, I went to several churches in Chengdu and Chongqing. Actually, it was this Zhihu question that caught my attention.

At that time in university, I was essentially a shut-in:

  • I didn’t like my roommates
  • I didn’t participate in any social activities
  • I could go more than a week without speaking, nearly aphasic

Additionally, when doing my daily rituals reading, I came across W.H. Auden (reference):

  • I envied his disciplined life
  • I also used supplements to boost my energy
  • I also saw that he converted from the left wing to Christianity in middle age

This sparked my curiosity, and from then on I planned to explore churches.

Catholicism vs. Protestantism

I won’t talk about Catholicism’s theology of suffering — early Sunday morning mass, kneeling on the kneeler for a full hour listening to the priest read scripture and preach, and being told to beg for mercy and salvation, that you have original sin… It completely failed to resonate.

But in Protestant churches, there is enviable joy and peace:

  • More kindness and smiles than I had seen all year in Chongqing
  • Full of pride and sincerity
  • Probably the idea that the saved should live a joyful, victorious, and abundant life

Actually, it’s more like:

  • A grassroots mutual-aid organization
  • A refuge for marginalized people

During worship, you can sing hymns with the organist and choir, and even the pastor’s sermons are very connected to daily life. At Chengdu’s Guangyin Church, I even heard the pastor praise the Chongqing mountain fire firefighters in a four-line regulated verse.

There are also:

  • Fellowship gatherings (even a singles youth fellowship)
  • Traditional arts classes: Chinese painting, calligraphy, hulusi
  • All festival and operational expenses are publicly disclosed
  • All deacons serve voluntarily
  • They can even help fellow believers find jobs or provide loans…

My Reflections

The deeper I went, the more amazing it felt. Compared to the blindly faithful fools and manipulative clergy I expected to see in literary works — apart from inherited scriptures and hymns, where is God?

I think this is the fascinating part: the Holy Spirit is not in that Heavenly Father above, but in the bonds of mutual aid and love between people. And rather than faith, the church members are more pragmatically using this structure; the real teaching has become a doctrine of selfless, unconditional divine love.

In reality, I only visited various churches and didn’t deeply enter their specific lives, so the more profound and moving insights came from Zhihu:

True Stories

Story One

My mother was a devout Buddhist for half her life, but unfortunately later developed cancer. Because long-term strict vegetarianism caused malnutrition, her ability to tolerate chemotherapy was extremely low, leaving the doctors in a difficult position. During a break between treatments, she went to a certain temple as a volunteer and for meditation, but the attitude of the “high monk” there toward this cancer patient chilled her.

Later, during her second hospitalization, in the last few days before her death, a pair of Australian friends flew back to visit her and, in the hospital room, transformed her from a Buddhist into a Christian. Mother passed away just a few days after converting. My father and I felt that since she was now a Christian, we should arrange the funeral in the Christian way. Since no one in our family was religious, we contacted the local church through friends’ help.

Mother passed away in the early morning, and the next morning a large group of strangers came from all over the city, young and old, men and women. There were college students with backpacks and white-haired old ladies. Dozens of people lined up neatly in the mourning hall, singing hymns and praying for this “sister in the Lord” who had converted just a few days before. After the ceremony, they each quietly left. Not to mention payment or reimbursement for transportation, they didn’t even drink a glass of water. For several dozen minutes, they quietly appeared and quietly left, not disturbing anyone else at the scene.

Afterwards, many relatives and friends present said they were deeply moved. Although I myself remain without faith to this day, my heart is full of gratitude to these kind strangers.

— Zhihu Answer

Story Two

Here’s the thing: my grandmother was a Christian during her lifetime.

However, not a single person in the family explicitly supported her belief, because first, her group didn’t look like any formal church; second, her emotional fluctuations during home worship were frightening (a bit like cursing and swearing); third, an elderly woman over seventy going out every day regardless of wind or rain raised safety concerns.

Later she fell ill, unable to straighten her back all day, in so much pain she couldn’t sleep, with a tumor growing in her spine. At first it was the size of an egg; when it was surgically removed, it filled most of a curved tray, and the wound on her back never healed until the day she died.

Her church friends came to see her spontaneously, not just to visit. They cared for my grandmother like their own parents, helping wipe her body, turn her over, and change her diapers. For our family, who was on the verge of collapse at the time, this was like sending charcoal in snowy weather. (It’s not that no one in our family was caring for her, but you can understand that caring for your mother and grandmother who was wailing day and night, sometimes comatose, with pain at every touch, while these church friends quietly and gently accompanied her, was a great comfort and help.)

Now our whole family agrees that these Christians are a group of great people, and we thank them.

— Zhihu Answer

[^gaokao]: The gaokao ( is China’s national college entrance examination, a grueling 2, day test that single-handedly determines which university you attend. Failing to get a good score can mean ending up at a school you’d never considered, as was the case for me.
[^bilibili]: Bilibili (B, is China’s leading video platform for anime, gaming, and creative content, often compared to a hybrid of YouTube and NicoNico, famous for its bullet comments that scroll across videos.
[^demidovich]: Demidovich ( refers to B.P. Demidovich’s Problems in Mathematical Analysis, a legendary Soviet-era problem book infamous among Chinese STEM students for its sheer volume of brutal calculus exercises, the mathematical equivalent of running stairs with a weighted vest.

entry.title: Wild Coop - A Spontaneous Poem Felt From the Audience
entry.content:
Wild Coop[^1]
Someone had already let out a cry through their instruments
A cry — I need only follow, straight to the soul
The soul, briefly breaking free from its shackles — let me free, even if just for tonight
Tonight — about the era, about choices, about life — they are revolutionaries of living, and I get a share too, their free, brave, passionate hearts
From the heart, a thought suddenly surfaced — I want to distribute gratitude everywhere, and then begin a new life


[^1]: Wild Coop (野外合作社, Yěwài Hézuòshè) — a Chinese indie rock band known for their raw, emotionally charged live performances. The name literally translates to “Wilderness Cooperative,” reflecting their grassroots, collaborative spirit.

entry.title: shanghaiqiutian - Miracle Workers
entry.content:
shanghaiqiutian
Tonight I visited Rome,
and in the city they are absolute Caesars
For his glory and victory, every citizen salutes him with fanatic fervor
He wields the power to make the crowd boil then fall silent, to make everyone squat down and then leap up together
But it comes not from authority — it comes from love
He embraces every member, then steps off the stage
The crowd parts for him like the Red Sea for Moses — he came to embrace everyone
In that moment I truly felt he was like a beam of light
If you ever get the chance, you must go see shanghaiqiutian — they are miracle workers still walking among us, their performances are a spiritual communion like a religious ritual
Dream fulfilled — last year my Bilibili[^bilibili] annual video was their live version (the CD version is too restrained)
Finally, much thanks to the new friend I met there — we just have tonight, thank you for being there!

💬 Comments

  • LoulanRE (2025-03-23 01:21): (Since they don’t like people taking photos and want everyone to just enjoy, I used internet photos

[^bilibili]: Bilibili (B站) is China’s leading video platform for anime, gaming, and creative content — often compared to a hybrid of YouTube and NicoNico, famous for its bullet comments (弹幕) that scroll across videos.
